原文: Rock music consists① of many different styles. Even though there is a common spirit② among all music groups, they make very different music. C. Rock music developed③ in the 1950s and the early 1960s. At that time the Beatles entered④ the world of music from Liverpool.
翻译: 摇滚乐包含许多不同的风格。尽管所有音乐团体之间有一种共同的精神，但他们创作的音乐却大不相同。C. 摇滚乐在20世纪50年代和60年代初发展起来。那时，披头士乐队从利物浦进入了音乐世界。

① consist /kənˈsɪst/ v. 由…组成，包含
Ⓡ consistent /kənˈsɪstənt/ adj. 一致的
Ⓡ consistency /kənˈsɪstənsi/ n. 一致性
Ⓡ consist of 由…组成
② spirit /ˈspɪrɪt/ n. 精神；情绪
Ⓡ spiritual /ˈspɪrɪtʃuəl/ adj. 精神的
Ⓡ inspire /ɪnˈspaɪə(r)/ v. 激励
Ⓡ inspiration /ˌɪnspəˈreɪʃn/ n. 灵感
③ develop /dɪˈveləp/ v. 发展；开发
Ⓡ development /dɪˈveləpmənt/ n. 发展
Ⓡ developing /dɪˈveləpɪŋ/ adj. 发展中的
Ⓡ developed /dɪˈveləpt/ adj. 发达的
④ enter /ˈentə(r)/ v. 进入；参加
Ⓡ entrance /ˈentrəns/ n. 入口；进入
Ⓡ entry /ˈentri/ n. 进入；条目

原文: After they were given an invitation⑤ to appear live⑥ on BBC, the Beatles quickly became famous⑦ in Britain with nationwide tours. By mid-1963, the Beatles had been extremely⑧ popular in England. B. Even their hairstyles became major⑨ trends⑩ at that time. They held large concerts and performed⑪ at clubs. They became the hottest thing on the pop music scene⑫ in England. They began as a modestly⑬ successful musician group and ended the year as show business legends(传说). John Lennon and Paul McCartney were named composers⑭ of the year.
翻译: 在收到BBC的邀请进行现场演出后，披头士乐队通过全国巡演迅速在英国成名。到1963年中，披头士乐队在英格兰已经极其受欢迎。B. 甚至他们的发型在当时也成为了主要潮流。他们举办大型音乐会并在俱乐部表演。他们成为了英格兰流行音乐界最热门的事物。他们开始时是一个适度成功的音乐团体，到年底却成为了演艺界的传奇。约翰·列侬和保罗·麦卡特尼被提名为年度作曲家。

Ⓡ inviting /ɪnˈvaɪtɪŋ/ adj. 诱人的
⑥ live /laɪv/ adj. 现场的 adv. 现场直播地
Ⓡ alive /əˈlaɪv/ adj. 活着的
Ⓡ lively /ˈlaɪvli/ adj. 活泼的
⑦ famous /ˈfeɪməs/ adj. 著名的
Ⓡ fame /feɪm/ n. 名声

⑨ major /ˈmeɪdʒə(r)/ adj. 主要的；重大的

⑫ scene /siːn/ n. 场景；界
Ⓡ scenery /ˈsiːnəri/ n. 风景
Ⓡ on the scene 在现场
⑬ modestly /ˈmɒdɪstli/ adv. 适度地；谦虚地
Ⓡ modest /ˈmɒdɪst/ adj. 适度的；谦虚的
Ⓡ modesty /ˈmɒdəsti/ n. 谦虚
⑭ composer /kəmˈpəʊzə(r)/ n. 作曲家

原文: A. They decided on a tour⑮ to the United States in 1964. They were not sure how the Americans would react⑯ to the new type of music. Beatlemania hit⑰ New York on February 7, 1964. Hundreds of fans jammed⑱ the airport to greet them. G. They performed their first concert in America at CBS television's 53rd street studio. The concert was broadcast⑲ live and attracted⑳ the largest one night audience㉑ in the history of television up to that time. The Beatles were described as a British invasion(入侵) by local and nationwide newspapers at that time. Their victory㉒ in America was still remembered as a major turning point㉓ in the history of rock and roll. Thanks to the Beatles, a lot of opportunities㉔ were opened up to new faces on the market. E. Many rock bands were able to follow in the footsteps㉕ of the Beatles.
翻译: A. 他们决定在1964年进行一次美国巡演。他们不确定美国人会对这种新类型的音乐作何反应。披头士狂热于1964年2月7日席卷纽约。数百名粉丝挤满机场迎接他们。G. 他们在CBS电视台第53街的演播室进行了在美国的第一场音乐会。这场音乐会进行了现场直播，并吸引了当时电视史上最大的单夜观众群。当时，地方和全国性报纸将披头士乐队描述为一次英国入侵。他们在美国的胜利仍被铭记为摇滚乐历史上的一个主要转折点。多亏了披头士乐队，市场上许多新面孔获得了大量机会。E. 许多摇滚乐队得以追随披头士的脚步。
必背词汇:
⑮ tour /tʊə(r)/ n. 巡演；旅行 v. 巡回
Ⓡ tourist /ˈtʊərɪst/ n. 游客
